module_map.responsibility: Main orchestrator: coordinates metadata collection, sorting, and display. Entry point for CLI runs.
File metadata collection from filesystem. Responsible for gathering and caching file properties (size, permissions, ownership, dates, git status, etc.)
CLI argument parsing and configuration. Defines display options, sorting criteria, and column selection.
Renders metadata to terminal output in grid or tree layout. Handles column rendering, padding, colors, icons, and formatting.
Sorts file entries based on configured criteria (name, size, date, extension, version).
Color and icon theming. Resolves colors based on file type, git status, and permissions; resolves icons from configured icon sets.
Reads and parses configuration files (TOML, YAML). Supplies defaults to flags if not overridden via CLI.
Git integration. Caches git status and repository information for file decoration.

data_flow.description: Reads filesystem via stat/lstat, collects file properties (size, permissions, dates, owner, type, links). Recursively collects child Meta objects for directories.
Orders Meta objects by configured SortType (name, size, date, etc.). Modifies metas in-place.
Maps Meta (file type, name, git status) to icon string based on theme rules and extension lookups.
Maps Meta (permissions, owner, git status, file type) to color code for rendering.

data_flow.key_function: inner_display_grid(display_option, metas, owner_cache, flags, colors, icons, ...) -> String
data_flow.description: Calls get_output for each Meta, assembles rows with aligned columns (Block enum determines which), applies colors/icons, renders to terminal-ready string.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Core hub: collected by core.rs fetch(), sorted by sort.rs, colored/iconed by theme/, and rendered by display.rs. Meta is the central data structure.
Color and icon resolution hub. Consumed by display.rs (get_output) and flags/color.rs for validation. Depends on config_file for theme definitions.
Configuration hub. Parsed by core.rs, controls sorting (sort.rs), layout (flags/layout.rs), and filtering (flags/blocks.rs). Consumed by display.rs for rendering decisions.

change_proposal.objective: Add a file checksum display column (SHA-256)
new_types: meta::Checksum (struct with sha256: String, computed: bool for lazy evaluation)
Block::Checksum variant in flags/blocks.rs enum Block
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow the pattern of meta/size.rs or meta/date.rs: create checksum.rs as a standalone submodule under meta/, implement lazy computation (only when requested in Blocks), use Option<Checksum> in Meta struct. In display.rs, follow get_output() pattern: add a match arm for Block::Checksum, call checksum.render() similar to Size::render_user().
change_proposal.integration_point: 1. Add Block::Checksum to flags/blocks.rs get_header(). 2. Add checksum: Option<Checksum> field to Meta struct (meta/mod.rs). 3. In Meta::from_path(), conditionally compute checksum if Block::Checksum is in active Blocks. 4. In display.rs get_output(), add match arm for Block::Checksum to call checksum.render() and populate padding_rules. 5. Update config_file.rs TOML schema to accept 'checksum' block option.
risks: Performance: SHA-256 computation on every file (especially large files) can be slow. Mitigated by lazy evaluation: only compute if Block::Checksum is requested.
Recursion depth: deep directory trees with checksums will accumulate I/O cost. Consider caching per-session.
Breaking change: new Block enum variant may affect downstream code assuming exhaustive pattern matching.
Platform differences: checksums are platform-independent but file access patterns differ (Windows vs POSIX). Test on both.

tool_usage.result: 52 files, 13223L, 672F, 87C (rust 100%); identified all top-level modules and submodule structure.
Meta struct with from_path(261L), recurse_into(63L), calculate_total_size(192L) key functions; 34 imports including filesystem and path utilities.
Core struct with new(), fetch(), sort(), display() orchestrating the pipeline; 24 imports for flags, meta, display, sort, and theme modules.
Display functions: inner_display_grid(82L), get_output(325L), inner_display_tree(232L) rendering metadata; 39 imports for colors, icons, formatting.
from_path called by recurse_into, fetch, core.rs run; calls expand_home, get_file_data, for_path for metadata collection.
inner_display_grid called by core run/display, calls get_output, render for output assembly.
get_output called by inner_display_grid and inner_display_tree, calls render, date_string, value_string for per-row rendering.
Block enum and Blocks config: configure_from(104L), from_config(157L), get_header(201L) for column management; 13 imports.
Block enum defined in flags/blocks.rs:186, referenced in display.rs for column rendering.

summary.findings: LSD is a modular Rust file lister with clear separation: meta/ collects file properties, flags/ parses configuration, theme/ resolves display attributes, display.rs renders rows, and core.rs orchestrates. Data flows from filesystem -> Meta -> sorting -> icon/color resolution -> display. Block enum controls which columns render.
summary.change_complexity: Medium. Adding SHA-256 column follows existing patterns (meta/size.rs, meta/date.rs). Requires new Block variant, lazy Checksum type, get_output() match arm, and integration in Meta::from_path(). Performance risk is high for large files/deep trees; recommend opt-in flag.
summary.estimated_lines: ~250 new lines (checksum.rs: 100L, display.rs: 30L, blocks.rs: 20L, meta/mod.rs: 50L, flags/display.rs: 50L)
